item.title: Gear News of the Week: There’s Yet Another New AI Browser, and Fujifilm Debuts the X-T30 III
item.description: Plus: Aura’s new digital photo frame goes wireless, a mood-morphing watch, Wyze and TP-Link unveil solar-powered outdoor security cameras, and Intel will open “AI Experience Stores” in five cities.
item.media:keywords: Shopping, Gear Roundup, Intel, artificial intelligence, security camera, art
item.dc:creator: Julian Chokkattu
item.dc:subject: Gear Roundup

item.title: Amazon Explains How Its AWS Outage Took Down the Web
item.description: Plus: The Jaguar Land Rover hack sets an expensive new record, OpenAI’s new Atlas browser raises security fears, Starlink cuts off scam compounds, and more.
item.media:keywords: security roundup, security, cybersecurity, Amazon, OpenAI, ChatGPT, artificial intelligence, privacy, data breach, hacking
item.dc:creator: Matt Burgess, Lily Hay Newman
item.dc:subject: Security Roundup

item.title: OpenAI's Sora Loses Its Top App Store Ranking to Dave's Hot Chicken
item.description: Dave’s Hot Chicken is the top app in the iOS App Store, ending Sora’s weeks-long reign.
item.media:keywords: Apps, App Store, food, OpenAI, artificial intelligence, chicken
item.dc:creator: Brian Barrett
item.dc:subject: Free Bird

item.title: How Data Centers Actually Work
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we discuss the economics and environmental impacts of energy-hungry data centers and whether these facilities are sustainable in the age of AI.
item.media:keywords: Uncanny Valley Podcast, podcasts, data centers, artificial intelligence
item.dc:creator: Lauren Goode, Michael Calore
item.dc:subject: Uncanny Valley

item.title: The Man Who Makes AI Slop by Hand
item.description: Chinese creator Tianran Mu went viral for mimicking the eerie, unsettling aesthetic of AI videos, but his work is 100 percent human.
item.media:keywords: Made in China, China, artificial intelligence, art, influencers, content creators
item.dc:creator: Zeyi Yang
item.dc:subject: Made in China

item.title: ChatGPT’s Horny Era Could Be Its Stickiest Yet
item.description: OpenAI will soon let adults create erotic content in ChatGPT. Experts say that could lead to “emotional commodification,” or horniness as a revenue stream.
item.media:keywords: artificial intelligence, machine learning, sex, OpenAI, ChatGPT, chatbots
item.dc:creator: Reece Rogers
item.dc:subject: System Load

item.title: AI Models Get Brain Rot, Too
item.description: A new study shows that feeding large language models low-quality, high-engagement content from social media lowers their cognitive abilities.
item.media:keywords: artificial intelligence, research, Social Media, X, TikTok, machine learning, algorithms
item.dc:creator: Will Knight
item.dc:subject: AI Lab

item.title: This Open Source Robot Brain Thinks in 3D
item.description: Open source language models are crucial to AI innovation. Can open robotics models do the same for physical machines?
item.media:keywords: artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, Social Media, robots, robotics
item.dc:creator: Will Knight
item.dc:subject: Machines Learning

item.title: People Who Say They’re Experiencing AI Psychosis Beg the FTC for Help
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission received 200 complaints mentioning ChatGPT between November 2022 and August 2025. Several attributed delusions, paranoia, and spiritual crises to the chatbot.
item.media:keywords: OpenAI, mental health, artificial intelligence, ChatGPT
item.dc:creator: Caroline Haskins
item.dc:subject: AI Hell

item.title: OpenAI’s Atlas Browser Takes Direct Aim at Google Chrome
item.description: The new ChatGPT-powered web browser is OpenAI’s boldest play yet to reinvent how people use the web.
item.media:keywords: OpenAI, Google, Browsers, artificial intelligence, Chrome
item.dc:creator: Reece Rogers
item.dc:subject: New Tab
